description: Add project type distinction (Software vs Non-Software), replace BRD with hierarchical requirements documents, build structured requirements panel UI, and add per-type admin context buckets.

userStories.title: Project type selection at creation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to select a project type (Software or Non-Software) when creating a new project so that the system structures my requirements appropriately
acceptanceCriteria: HeroSection shows heading, subtext, and 'New Project' button (no textarea)
NewProjectModal shows two selectable cards: Software and Non-Software
Create button disabled until type selected
POST /api/projects/ includes project_type field (required)
createProject endpoint no longer requires first_message
Navigates to /project/:id after creation
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § projects — project_type already exists (M18)
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § Modal / Dialog
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § [T-3.1.01, T-3.1.02, T-3.1.03]
Files: frontend/src/components/landing/HeroSection.tsx, NewProjectModal.tsx (new), use-create-project.ts, services/gateway/apps/projects/views.py, serializers.py
Gotchas: project_type field exists — no migration needed. first_message optional for backwards compat.

components: NewProjectModal — two cards, disabled create button
test_cases: T-3.1.01 | Unit | Modal shows two cards

userStories.title: Structured requirements data model
userStories.description: As a developer, I want hierarchical JSON requirements models supporting Software (Epics/Stories) and Non-Software (Milestones/Packages) structures
acceptanceCriteria: RequirementsDocumentDraft model created (id, project_id UNIQUE, title, short_description, structure JSONB, item_locks JSONB, allow_information_gaps, readiness_evaluation JSONB)
RequirementsDocumentVersion model created (id, project_id, version_number, title, short_description, structure JSONB, pdf_file_path, UNIQUE(project_id, version_number))
Migration creates tables, migrates brd_drafts/brd_versions, drops old tables
Structure validation enforces correct schema per project_type
API endpoints: GET/PATCH /requirements/, POST/PATCH/DELETE items and children, POST reorder, POST generate
WebSocket events: requirements_updated, requirements_generating, requirements_ready
gRPC RPCs: GetRequirementsState, UpdateRequirementsStructure
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § requirements_document_drafts, requirements_document_versions
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Requirements Management
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § [T-3.2.01, T-3.2.02, T-3.2.03, API-3.01, API-3.02]
Files: services/core/apps/projects/models.py, migrations/0005_requirements_documents.py (new), services/gateway/apps/projects/serializers.py, views.py, websocket/consumers.py, proto/core.proto, grpc_clients/
Gotchas: Migration maps old BRD 6-section model to new structure. Structure JSONB validated against project_type. WebSocket group project_{id} exists.

data_model: requirements_document_drafts: id, project_id UNIQUE, structure jsonb (epics/milestones), item_locks jsonb
requirements_document_versions: id, project_id, version_number, structure jsonb, UNIQUE(project_id, version_number)
test_cases: T-3.2.01 | Unit | Software structure validation passes
API-3.01 | Integration | Add epic broadcasts ws:requirements_updated

userStories.title: Structured requirements panel UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want a visual panel showing hierarchical requirements (Epics/Stories or Milestones/Packages) alongside chat
acceptanceCriteria: RequirementsPanel shows title, description, contributors, items list
EpicCard/MilestoneCard: collapsible accordion, inline edit, delete, drag handle
UserStoryCard/WorkPackageCard: compact card, criteria/deliverables, drag handle
Drag-and-drop using @dnd-kit (stories move between epics, packages between milestones)
WebSocket real-time sync on requirements_updated event
WorkspaceLayout: 40/60 split (chat/requirements) with draggable divider
Optimistic updates with server reconciliation
Last-write-wins conflict resolution with toast
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § Requirements Panel Components
Design: docs/03-design/page-layouts.md § Project Workspace — 40/60 split
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § WebSocket Events
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § [T-3.3.01, T-3.3.02, T-3.3.03, E2E-3.01]
Files: frontend/src/components/workspace/RequirementsPanel.tsx (new), EpicCard.tsx, MilestoneCard.tsx, UserStoryCard.tsx, WorkPackageCard.tsx, RequirementsItemEditor.tsx, WorkspaceLayout.tsx, use-websocket.ts, api/projects.ts, package.json (add @dnd-kit)
Gotchas: @dnd-kit needed. Multi-user conflict uses last-write-wins. WebSocket payload includes full structure.

components: RequirementsPanel — title, desc, contributors, items with 'Add' button
UserStoryCard/WorkPackageCard — compact card, criteria chips, priority
WorkspaceLayout — split panel, draggable divider (40/60 default)
test_cases: T-3.3.01 | Unit | Software project shows EpicCard
T-3.3.02 | Unit | Drag story between epics calls reorder
E2E-3.01 | E2E | Last write wins, toast on conflict

userStories.title: Admin context buckets per project type
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to manage AI context separately for global, software, and non-software projects
acceptanceCriteria: facilitator_context_bucket has context_type column CHECK (global|software|non_software), UNIQUE on context_type
context_agent_bucket has same context_type column and constraints
Migration adds context_type, seeds 3 rows per bucket (global, software, non_software)
API endpoints: GET/PATCH /admin/ai-context/facilitator?type=, /company?type=
AIContextTab: segmented control (Global/Software/Non-Software), shows facilitator + company editors per type
Embedding pipeline tags chunks with context_type
Context retrieval combines global + type-specific chunks based on project.project_type
Facilitator context assembly combines global + type-specific buckets
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes:
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/data-model.md § facilitator_context_bucket, context_agent_bucket
Architecture: docs/02-architecture/api-design.md § Admin AI Context
Design: docs/03-design/component-specs.md § Tabs
AI: docs/03-ai/agent-architecture.md § Context Bucket Structure
Testing: docs/04-test-architecture/test-matrix.md § [T-3.4.01, T-3.4.02, API-3.03]
Files: services/ai/apps/ai/models.py, migrations/0002_add_context_type.py (new), services/gateway/apps/admin/views.py, serializers.py, frontend/src/features/admin/AIContextTab.tsx, services/ai/processing/context_assembler.py, embedding/chunker.py, reindexer.py
Gotchas: AI models may be in services/ai/apps/ai/models.py. Migration seeds 3 rows per bucket. RAG query filters context_type IN (global, project_type).

data_model: facilitator_context_bucket: context_type varchar(20) CHECK UNIQUE — 3 rows
context_chunks: add context_type varchar(20) for tagging
test_cases: T-3.4.01 | Unit | UNIQUE constraint on context_type
API-3.03 | Integration | Retrieval combines global + project_type chunks
ai_specs: RAG — filter by context_type IN (global, project_type)
